title: DSDS Blueprint Definitions
description: Defines documents for blueprints. A blueprint is a pre-composed block of code that represents a ready-to-use pattern in the design system — a copy-paste starting point built from the system's components. A blueprint document holds the identity of the block, the rules for using it (`guidelines`), when it fits (`useCases`), and the code itself (`code`). Blueprints sit at the same level as components. Where a component documents a single building block, a blueprint documents a composition of building blocks captured as code.

blueprint.description: A pre-composed block of code that captures a design system pattern. Blueprints give consumers a working starting point assembled from system components — for example, a layout, a settings form, or a confirmation dialog. The top level holds its identity (`identifier`, `name`, optional `description`), the `code` block that consumers copy, `guidelines` for how to use and adapt it, and `useCases` for when it is the right choice. Blueprints are intentionally simple: they document a code composition, not the full anatomy/API surface a component carries.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a blueprint.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier for the blueprint (e.g., 'search-bar', 'settings-form', 'confirmation-dialog'). MUST be lowercase kebab-case and unique within its entity group.

name.description: Display name shown in docs (e.g., 'Search bar', 'Settings form', 'Confirmation dialog').

description.description: What this blueprint is, the pattern it captures, and which components it composes. CommonMark supported.

code.description: The pre-composed block of code consumers copy to use the blueprint. Holds the source itself (`code`) and the language or syntax it is written in (`language`).
The source code of the blueprint — the block consumers copy and adapt.

language.description: The programming language or syntax of the code (e.g., 'jsx', 'tsx', 'html', 'css', 'vue', 'svelte').

guidelines.description: Actionable rules for using and adapting the blueprint. Each item pairs a guidance statement with a rationale and an RFC 2119 enforcement level. Reuses the shared guideline-entry shape so blueprint rules read the same as guidelines elsewhere in the system.

useCases.description: Scenario-driven use cases describing when this blueprint is — or is not — the right choice. Each entry is marked 'recommended' or 'discouraged'. Reuses the shared use-case shape. Authors SHOULD lead with recommended scenarios.

metadata.description: Optional metadata fields, keyed by name: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links. Use the `links` field to point at the components this blueprint composes.
